verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est verbum Domini ad me, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֥י דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֵלַ֥י לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the word of the Lord came to me, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Herran sana tapahtui minulle ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: The word of the Lord came again unto me, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu ergo, fili hominis, assume super Tyrum lamentum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַתָּ֣ה בֶן־אָדָ֔ם שָׂ֥א עַל־צֹ֖ר קִינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou therefore, O son of man, take up a lamentation for Tyre:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä ihmisen poika, tee valitusitku Tyrosta,
verses.king_james: Now, thou son of man, take up a lamentation for Tyrus;

verses.latin_vulgate: et dices Tyro, quæ habitat in introitu maris, negotiationi populorum ad insulas multas :   Hæc dicit Dominus Deus :  O Tyre, tu dixisti : Perfecti decoris ego sum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָמַרְתָּ֣ לְצ֗וֹר (הישבתי) [הַיֹּשֶׁ֙בֶת֙] עַל־מְבוֹאֹ֣ת יָ֔ם רֹכֶ֙לֶת֙ הָֽעַמִּ֔ים אֶל־אִיִּ֖ים רַבִּ֑ים {ס} כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ אֲדֹנָ֣י יֱהֹוִ֔ה צ֕וֹר אַ֣תְּ אָמַ֔רְתְּ אֲנִ֖י כְּלִ֥ילַת יֹֽפִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And say to Tyre that dwelleth at the entry of the sea, being the mart of the people for many islands: Thus saith the Lord God: O Tyre, thou hast said: I am of perfect beauty,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sano Tyrolle, joka asuu meren tykönä ja tekee kauppaa monen luodon kansan kanssa: näin sanoo Herra, Herra: o Tyro, sinä olet sanonut: minä olen kaikkein kaunein.
verses.king_james: And say unto Tyrus, O thou that art situate at the entry of the sea, which art a merchant of the people for many isles, Thus saith the Lord God; O Tyrus, thou hast said, I am of perfect beauty.

verses.latin_vulgate: et in corde maris sita.  Finitimi tui qui te ædificaverunt, impleverunt decorem tuum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּלֵ֥ב יַמִּ֖ים גְּבוּלָ֑יִךְ בֹּנַ֕יִךְ כָּלְל֖וּ יׇפְיֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And situate in the heart of the sea. Thy neighbours, that built thee, have perfected thy beauty:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun rajas ovat keskellä merta; ja sinun rakentajas ovat valmistaneet sinun kaikkein kauneimmaksi.
verses.king_james: Thy borders are in the midst of the seas, thy builders have perfected thy beauty.

verses.latin_vulgate: abietibus de Sanir exstruxerunt te cum omnibus tabulatis maris :  cedrum de Libano tulerunt ut facerent tibi malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרוֹשִׁ֤ים מִשְּׂנִיר֙ בָּ֣נוּ לָ֔ךְ אֵ֖ת כׇּל־לֻחֹתָ֑יִם אֶ֤רֶז מִלְּבָנוֹן֙ לָקָ֔חוּ לַעֲשׂ֥וֹת תֹּ֖רֶן עָלָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: With fir trees of Sanir they have built thee with all sea planks: they have taken cedars from Libanus to make thee masts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki sinun lautas on hongasta Seniristä, ja olet antanut tuoda sedripuita Libanonista tehdäkses sinulles haahden pielen.
verses.king_james: They have made all thy ship boards of fir trees of Senir: they have taken cedars from Lebanon to make masts for thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quercus de Basan dolaverunt in remos tuos,  et transtra tua fecerunt tibi ex ebore indico,  et prætoriola de insulis Italiæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַלּוֹנִים֙ מִבָּשָׁ֔ן עָשׂ֖וּ מִשּׁוֹטָ֑יִךְ קַרְשֵׁ֤ךְ עָֽשׂוּ־שֵׁן֙ בַּת־אֲשֻׁרִ֔ים מֵאִיֵּ֖י כִּתִּיִּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: They have cut thy oars out of the oaks of Basan: and they have made thee benches of Indian ivory and cabins with things brought from the islands of Italy.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun airos olivat tehdyt Basanin tammesta, sinun piittas elephantin luista, Kittimin luodoista:
verses.king_james: Of the oaks of Bashan have they made thine oars; the company of the Ashurites have made thy benches of ivory, brought out of the isles of Chittim.

verses.latin_vulgate: Byssus varia de Ægypto texta est tibi in velum  ut poneretur in malo :  hyacinthus et purpura de insulis Elisa  facta sunt operimentum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֵׁשׁ־בְּרִקְמָ֤ה מִמִּצְרַ֙יִם֙ הָיָ֣ה מִפְרָשֵׂ֔ךְ לִֽהְי֥וֹת לָ֖ךְ לְנֵ֑ס תְּכֵ֧לֶת וְאַרְגָּמָ֛ן מֵאִיֵּ֥י אֱלִישָׁ֖ה הָיָ֥ה מְכַסֵּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Fine broidered linen from Egypt was woven for thy sail, to be spread on thy mast: blue and purple from the islands of Elisa, were made thy covering.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun purjees oli Egyptin kalliista liinasta neulottu, sinulle merkiksi; sinun peittees oli sinivilloista ja Elisan luotoin purpurasta.
verses.king_james: Fine linen with broidered work from Egypt was that which thou spreadest forth to be thy sail; blue and purple from the isles of Elishah was that which covered thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habitatores Sidonis et Aradii fuerunt remiges tui :  sapientes tui, Tyre, facti sunt gubernatores tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֹשְׁבֵ֤י צִידוֹן֙ וְאַרְוַ֔ד הָי֥וּ שָׁטִ֖ים לָ֑ךְ חֲכָמַ֤יִךְ צוֹר֙ הָ֣יוּ בָ֔ךְ הֵ֖מָּה חֹבְלָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The inhabitants of Sidon, and the Arabians were thy rowers: thy wise men, O Tyre, were thy pilots.
verses.finnish_biblia: Zidonista ja Arvadista olivat sinun haaksimiehes; ja sinulla olivat toimelliset miehet Tyrosta, haahden haltiat.
verses.king_james: The inhabitants of Zidon and Arvad were thy mariners: thy wise men, O Tyrus, that were in thee, were thy pilots.

verses.latin_vulgate: Senes Giblii et prudentes ejus  habuerunt nautas ad ministerium variæ supellectilis tuæ :  omnes naves maris, et nautæ earum,  fuerunt in populo negotiationis tuæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זִקְנֵ֨י גְבַ֤ל וַחֲכָמֶ֙יהָ֙ הָ֣יוּ בָ֔ךְ מַחֲזִיקֵ֖י בִּדְקֵ֑ךְ כׇּל־אֳנִיּ֨וֹת הַיָּ֤ם וּמַלָּֽחֵיהֶם֙ הָ֣יוּ בָ֔ךְ לַעֲרֹ֖ב מַעֲרָבֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The ancients of Gebal, and the wise men thereof furnished mariners for the service of thy various furniture: all the ships of the sea, and their mariners were thy factors.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vanhimmat ja taitavat Gebalista paransivat sinun laivas. Kaikki laivat meressä ja haaksimiehet löyttiin sinun tykönäs; he tekivät kauppaa sinussa.
verses.king_james: The ancients of Gebal and the wise men thereof were in thee thy calkers: all the ships of the sea with their mariners were in thee to occupy thy merchandise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Persæ, et Lydii, et Libyes erant in exercitu tuo  viri bellatores tui :  clypeum et galeam suspenderunt in te pro ornatu tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּרַ֨ס וְל֤וּד וּפוּט֙ הָי֣וּ בְחֵילֵ֔ךְ אַנְשֵׁ֖י מִלְחַמְתֵּ֑ךְ מָגֵ֤ן וְכוֹבַע֙ תִּלּוּ־בָ֔ךְ הֵ֖מָּה נָתְנ֥וּ הֲדָרֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Persians, and Lydians, and the Libyans were thy soldiers in thy army: they hung up the buckler and the helmet in thee for thy ornament.
verses.finnish_biblia: Persiasta, Lydiasta ja Lybiasta olivat urhoolliset miehet sinun sotajoukossas; jotka kilpensä ja otansa sinussa ripustivat, ne sinun kaunistivat.
verses.king_james: They of Persia and of Lud and of Phut were in thine army, thy men of war: they hanged the shield and helmet in thee; they set forth thy comeliness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii Aradii cum exercitu tuo erant super muros tuos in circuitu :  sed et Pigmæi qui erant in turribus tuis,  pharetras suas suspenderunt in muris tuis per gyrum :  ipsi compleverunt pulchritudinem tuam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּנֵ֧י אַרְוַ֣ד וְחֵילֵ֗ךְ עַל־חוֹמוֹתַ֙יִךְ֙ סָבִ֔יב וְגַ֨מָּדִ֔ים בְּמִגְדְּלוֹתַ֖יִךְ הָי֑וּ שִׁלְטֵיהֶ֞ם תִּלּ֤וּ עַל־חוֹמוֹתַ֙יִךְ֙ סָבִ֔יב הֵ֖מָּה כָּלְל֥וּ יׇפְיֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The men of Arad were with thy army upon thy walls round about: the Pygmeans also that were in thy towers, hung up their quivers on thy walls round about: they perfected thy beauty.
verses.finnish_biblia: Arvadin lapset olivat sinun sotajoukossas, kaikki ympäri sinun muurias, ja vartiat sinun tornissas. He ripustivat kilpensä sinun muuris päälle kaikki ympäri; ne tekivät niin sinun ihanaksi.
verses.king_james: The men of Arvad with thine army were upon thy walls round about, and the Gammadims were in thy towers: they hanged their shields upon thy walls round about; they have made thy beauty perfect.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carthaginenses negotiatores tui,  a multitudine cunctarum divitiarum,  argento, ferro, stanno, plumboque  repleverunt nundinas tuas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּרְשִׁ֥ישׁ סֹחַרְתֵּ֖ךְ מֵרֹ֣ב כׇּל־ה֑וֹן בְּכֶ֤סֶף בַּרְזֶל֙ בְּדִ֣יל וְעוֹפֶ֔רֶת נָתְנ֖וּ עִזְבוֹנָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Carthaginians thy merchants supplied thy fairs with a multitude of all kinds of riches, with silver, iron, tin, and lead,
verses.finnish_biblia: Tarsis teki kauppaa sinun kanssas kaikkinaisen kalun paljoudella: hopialla, raudalla, tinalla ja lyijyllä, joita he toivat sinun markkinoilles.
verses.king_james: Tarshish was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of all kind of riches; with silver, iron, tin, and lead, they traded in thy fairs.

verses.latin_vulgate: Græcia, Thubal, et Mosoch, ipsi institores tui :  mancipia, et vasa ærea advexerunt populo tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָוָ֤ן תֻּבַל֙ וָמֶ֔שֶׁךְ הֵ֖מָּה רֹכְלָ֑יִךְ בְּנֶ֤פֶשׁ אָדָם֙ וּכְלֵ֣י נְחֹ֔שֶׁת נָתְנ֖וּ מַעֲרָבֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Greece, Thubal, and Mosoch, they were thy merchants, they brought to thy people slaves and vessels of brass.
verses.finnish_biblia: Javan, Tubal ja Mesek teki kauppaa sinun kanssas, ja toivat ihmisiä ja vaskiastioita sinun kaupalles.
verses.king_james: Javan, Tubal, and Meshech, they were thy merchants: they traded the persons of men and vessels of brass in thy market.

verses.latin_vulgate: De domo Thogorma, equos, et equites, et mulos  adduxerunt ad forum tuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִבֵּ֖ית תּוֹגַרְמָ֑ה סוּסִ֤ים וּפָרָשִׁים֙ וּפְרָדִ֔ים נָתְנ֖וּ עִזְבוֹנָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: From the house of Thogorma they brought horses, and horsemen, and mules to thy market.
verses.finnish_biblia: Togarmasta toivat he hevosia ja vaunuja ja muuleja sinun markkinoilles.
verses.king_james: They of the house of Togarmah traded in thy fairs with horses and horsemen and mules.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii Dedan negotiatores tui ;  insulæ multæ, negotiatio manus tuæ :  dentes eburneos et hebeninos commutaverunt in pretio tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּנֵ֤י דְדָן֙ רֹכְלַ֔יִךְ אִיִּ֥ים רַבִּ֖ים סְחֹרַ֣ת יָדֵ֑ךְ קַרְנ֥וֹת שֵׁן֙ (והובנים) [וְהׇבְנִ֔ים] הֵשִׁ֖יבוּ אֶשְׁכָּרֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The men of Dedan were thy merchants: many islands were the traffic of thy hand, they exchanged for thy price teeth of ivory and ebony.
verses.finnish_biblia: Dedanin lapset olivat sinun kauppamiehes, ja sinä teit kauppaa joka paikassa luotokunnissa; he antoivat sinulle elephantin luita ja Hebenpuita hinnaksi.
verses.king_james: The men of Dedan were thy merchants; many isles were the merchandise of thine hand: they brought thee for a present horns of ivory and ebony.

verses.latin_vulgate: Syrus negotiator tuus propter multitudinem operum tuorum :  gemmam, et purpuram, et scutulata,  et byssum, et sericum, et chodchod  proposuerunt in mercatu tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲרָ֥ם סֹחַרְתֵּ֖ךְ מֵרֹ֣ב מַעֲשָׂ֑יִךְ בְּ֠נֹ֠פֶךְ אַרְגָּמָ֨ן וְרִקְמָ֤ה וּבוּץ֙ וְרָאמֹ֣ת וְכַדְכֹּ֔ד נָתְנ֖וּ בְּעִזְבוֹנָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The Syrian was thy merchant: by reason of the multitude of thy works, they set forth precious stories, and purple, and broidered works, and fine linen, and silk, and chodchod in thy market.
verses.finnish_biblia: Syrialaiset noutivat sinun töitäs, joitas teit, toivat rubiinia, purppuraa, tapeteja, silkkiä ja samettia, ja kristallia sinun markkinoilles.
verses.king_james: Syria was thy merchant by reason of the multitude of the wares of thy making: they occupied in thy fairs with emeralds, purple, and broidered work, and fine linen, and coral, and agate.

verses.latin_vulgate: Juda et terra Israël, ipsi institores tui in frumento primo :  balsamum, et mel, et oleum, et resinam  proposuerunt in nundinis tuis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהוּדָה֙ וְאֶ֣רֶץ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל הֵ֖מָּה רֹכְלָ֑יִךְ בְּחִטֵּ֣י מִ֠נִּ֠ית וּפַנַּ֨ג וּדְבַ֤שׁ וָשֶׁ֙מֶן֙ וָצֹ֔רִי נָתְנ֖וּ מַעֲרָבֵֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Juda and the land of Israel, they were thy merchants with the best corn: they set forth balm, and honey, and oil and rosin in thy fairs.
verses.finnish_biblia: Juuda ja Israelin maa teki myös kauppaa sinun kanssas, ja toi sinun markkinoilles nisuja Minnitistä, ja balsamia ja hunajaa, ja öljyä ja mastiksia.
verses.king_james: Judah, and the land of Israel, they were thy merchants: they traded in thy market wheat of Minnith, and Pannag, and honey, and oil, and balm.

verses.latin_vulgate: Damascenus negotiator tuus in multitudine operum tuorum,  in multitudine diversarum opum,  in vino pingui, in lanis coloris optimi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דַּמֶּ֧שֶׂק סֹחַרְתֵּ֛ךְ בְּרֹ֥ב מַעֲשַׂ֖יִךְ מֵרֹ֣ב כׇּל־ה֑וֹן בְּיֵ֥ין חֶלְבּ֖וֹן וְצֶ֥מֶר צָֽחַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: The men of Damascus were thy merchants in the multitude of thy works, the multitude of divers riches, in rich wine, in wool of the best colour.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nouti myös Damasku sinun töitäs sinusta paljoudessa, ja paljon kaikkinaisia kaluja, väkevän viinan ja kalliin villan edestä.
verses.king_james: Damascus was thy merchant in the multitude of the wares of thy making, for the multitude of all riches; in the wine of Helbon, and white wool.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dan, et Græcia, et Mosel,  in nundinis tuis proposuerunt ferrum fabrefactum :  stacte et calamus in negotiatione tua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדָ֤ן וְיָוָן֙ מְאוּזָּ֔ל בְּעִזְבוֹנַ֖יִךְ נָתָ֑נּוּ בַּרְזֶ֤ל עָשׁוֹת֙ קִדָּ֣ה וְקָנֶ֔ה בְּמַעֲרָבֵ֖ךְ הָיָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Dan, and Greece, and Mosel have set forth in thy marts wrought iron: stacte, and calamus were in thy market.
verses.finnish_biblia: Dan ja Javanmehusal toivat myös sinun markkinoilles rautakalua, kassia- ja kalmus-yrttejä sinun kauppaa tehdäkses.
verses.king_james: Dan also and Javan going to and fro occupied in thy fairs: bright iron, cassia, and calamus, were in thy market.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dedan institores tui in tapetibus ad sedendum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דְּדָן֙ רֹֽכַלְתֵּ֔ךְ בְּבִגְדֵי־חֹ֖פֶשׁ לְרִכְבָּֽהֿ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The men of Dedan were thy merchants in tapestry for seats.
verses.finnish_biblia: Dedan osti sinulta kalliita vaatteita, joiden päällä ajoissa istutaan.
verses.king_james: Dedan was thy merchant in precious clothes for chariots.

verses.latin_vulgate: Arabia et universi principes Cedar,  ipsi negotiatores manus tuæ :  cum agnis, et arietibus, et hædis,  venerunt ad te negotiatores tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲרַב֙ וְכׇל־נְשִׂיאֵ֣י קֵדָ֔ר הֵ֖מָּה סֹחֲרֵ֣י יָדֵ֑ךְ בְּכָרִ֤ים וְאֵילִם֙ וְעַתּוּדִ֔ים בָּ֖ם סֹחֲרָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Arabia, and all the princes of Cedar, they were the merchants of thy hand: thy merchants came to thee with lambs, and rams, and kids.
verses.finnish_biblia: Atabia ja kaikki Kebarin ruhtinaat kaupitsivat sinulle lampaita, oinaita ja kauriita; niillä he tekivät sinun kanssas kauppaa.
verses.king_james: Arabia, and all the princes of Kedar, they occupied with thee in lambs, and rams, and goats: in these were they thy merchants.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venditores Saba et Reema, ipsi negotiatores tui :  cum universis primis aromatibus, et lapide pretioso, et auro,  quod proposuerunt in mercatu tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רֹכְלֵ֤י שְׁבָא֙ וְרַעְמָ֔ה הֵ֖מָּה רֹכְלָ֑יִךְ בְּרֹ֨אשׁ כׇּל־בֹּ֜שֶׂם וּבְכׇל־אֶ֤בֶן יְקָרָה֙ וְזָהָ֔ב נָתְנ֖וּ עִזְבוֹנָֽיִךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The sellers of Saba, and Reema, they were thy merchants: with all the best spices, and precious stones, and gold, which they set forth in thy market.
verses.finnish_biblia: Saban ja Ragman kauppamiehet tekivät sinun kanssas kauppaa, ja toivat sinun markkinoilles kaikkinaisia kalliita yrttejä, kalliita kiviä ja kultaa.
verses.king_james: The merchants of Sheba and Raamah, they were thy merchants: they occupied in thy fairs with chief of all spices, and with all precious stones, and gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Haran, et Chene, et Eden, negotiatores tui ;  Saba, Assur, et Chelmad venditores tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָרָ֤ן וְכַנֵּה֙ וָעֶ֔דֶן רֹכְלֵ֖י שְׁבָ֑א אַשּׁ֖וּר כִּלְמַ֥ד רֹכַלְתֵּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Haran, and Chene, and Eden were thy merchants; Saba, Assur, and Chelmad sold to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Haran ja Kanne, ja Eden ynnä Seban kauppamiesten kanssa, Assur ja Kilmad olivat myös sinun kauppamiehes.
verses.king_james: Haran, and Canneh, and Eden, the merchants of Sheba, Asshur, and Chilmad, were thy merchants.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi negotiatores tui multifariam,  involucris hyacinthi, et polymitorum,  gazarumque pretiosarum,  quæ obvolutæ et astrictæ erant funibus :  cedros quoque habebant in negotiationibus tuis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤מָּה רֹכְלַ֙יִךְ֙ בְּמַכְלֻלִ֔ים בִּגְלוֹמֵי֙ תְּכֵ֣לֶת וְרִקְמָ֔ה וּבְגִנְזֵ֖י בְּרֹמִ֑ים בַּחֲבָלִ֧ים חֲבֻשִׁ֛ים וַאֲרֻזִ֖ים בְּמַרְכֻלְתֵּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: They were thy merchants in divers manners, with bales of blue cloth, and of embroidered work, and of precious riches, which were wrapped up and bound with cords: they had cedars also in thy merchandise.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki nämät kaupitsivat sinulle kalliita vaatteita, silkkiä ja neulotuita hameita, jotka he kalliissa sederarkuissa hyvin tallella pantuna toivat sinun markkinoilles.
verses.king_james: These were thy merchants in all sorts of things, in blue clothes, and broidered work, and in chests of rich apparel, bound with cords, and made of cedar, among thy merchandise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Naves maris, principes tui in negotiatione tua :  et repleta es, et glorificata nimis in corde maris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֳנִיּ֣וֹת תַּרְשִׁ֔ישׁ שָׁרוֹתַ֖יִךְ מַעֲרָבֵ֑ךְ וַתִּמָּלְאִ֧י וַֽתִּכְבְּדִ֛י מְאֹ֖ד בְּלֵ֥ב יַמִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: The ships of the sea, were thy chief in thy merchandise: and thou wast replenished, and glorified exceedingly in the heart of the sea.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Tarsiksen laivat olivat ylimmäiset sinun kaupassas; niin sinä olet peräti rikkaaksi ja jaloksi tullut keskellä merta.
verses.king_james: The ships of Tarshish did sing of thee in thy market: and thou wast replenished, and made very glorious in the midst of the seas.

verses.latin_vulgate: In aquis multis adduxerunt te remiges tui :  ventus auster contrivit te in corde maris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּמַ֤יִם רַבִּים֙ הֱבִא֔וּךְ הַשָּׁטִ֖ים אֹתָ֑ךְ ר֚וּחַ הַקָּדִ֔ים שְׁבָרֵ֖ךְ בְּלֵ֥ב יַמִּֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thy rowers have brought thee into great waters: the south wind hath broken thee in the heart of the sea.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sinun haaksimiehes toivat sinun suurilla vesillä; mutta itätuulen pitää sinun musertaman rikki keskellä merta:
verses.king_james: Thy rowers have brought thee into great waters: the east wind hath broken thee in the midst of the seas.

verses.latin_vulgate: Divitiæ tuæ, et thesauri tui,  et multiplex instrumentum tuum :  nautæ tui et gubernatores tui,  qui tenebant supellectilem tuam,  et populo tuo præerant :  viri quoque bellatores tui, qui erant in te,  cum universa multitudine tua quæ est in medio tui,  cadent in corde maris in die ruinæ tuæ :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הוֹנֵךְ֙ וְעִזְבוֹנַ֔יִךְ מַעֲרָבֵ֕ךְ מַלָּחַ֖יִךְ וְחֹבְלָ֑יִךְ מַחֲזִיקֵ֣י בִדְקֵ֣ךְ וְֽעֹרְבֵ֣י מַ֠עֲרָבֵ֠ךְ וְכׇל־אַנְשֵׁ֨י מִלְחַמְתֵּ֜ךְ אֲשֶׁר־בָּ֗ךְ וּבְכׇל־קְהָלֵךְ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּתוֹכֵ֔ךְ יִפְּלוּ֙ בְּלֵ֣ב יַמִּ֔ים בְּי֖וֹם מַפַּלְתֵּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thy riches, and thy treasures, and thy manifold furniture, thy mariners, and thy pilots, who kept thy goods, and were chief over thy people: thy men of war also, that were in thee, with all thy multitude that is in the midst of thee: shall fall in the heart of the sea in the day of thy ruin.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin että sinun tavaras, kauppakalus, ostajas, haaksimiehes, laivain haltias, laivain rakentajas ja sinun kaupitsias, ja kaikki sinun sotamiehes, ja kaikki kanssa sinussa pitää hukkuman keskellä merta, sinun lankeemises päivänä;
verses.king_james: Thy riches, and thy fairs, thy merchandise, thy mariners, and thy pilots, thy calkers, and the occupiers of thy merchandise, and all thy men of war, that are in thee, and in all thy company which is in the midst of thee, shall fall into the midst of the seas in the day of thy ruin.

verses.latin_vulgate: a sonitu clamoris gubernatorum tuorum conturbabuntur classes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְק֖וֹל זַעֲקַ֣ת חֹבְלָ֑יִךְ יִרְעֲשׁ֖וּ מִגְרֹשֽׁוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thy fleets shall be troubled at the sound of the cry of thy pilots.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin että satamatkin pitää vapiseman sinun haahteis haltiain huudosta.
verses.king_james: The suburbs shall shake at the sound of the cry of thy pilots.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et descendent de navibus suis omnes qui tenebant remum :  nautæ et universi gubernatores maris in terra stabunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽיָרְד֞וּ מֵאֳנִיּֽוֹתֵיהֶ֗ם כֹּ֚ל תֹּפְשֵׂ֣י מָשׁ֔וֹט מַלָּחִ֕ים כֹּ֖ל חֹבְלֵ֣י הַיָּ֑ם אֶל־הָאָ֖רֶץ יַעֲמֹֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all that handled the oar shall come down from their ships: the mariners, and all the pilots of the sea shall stand upon the land:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki, jotka soutavat haaksimiesten ja haahtein haltiain kanssa, pitää laivoista menemän maalle seisomaan,
verses.king_james: And all that handle the oar, the mariners, and all the pilots of the sea, shall come down from their ships, they shall stand upon the land;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ejulabunt super te voce magna :  et clamabunt amare,  et superjacient pulverem capitibus suis,  et cinere conspergentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִשְׁמִ֤יעוּ עָלַ֙יִךְ֙ בְּקוֹלָ֔ם וְיִזְעֲק֖וּ מָרָ֑ה וְיַעֲל֤וּ עָפָר֙ עַל־רָ֣אשֵׁיהֶ֔ם בָּאֵ֖פֶר יִתְפַּלָּֽשׁוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they shall mourn over thee with a loud voice and shall cry bitterly: and they shall cast up dust upon their heads and shall be sprinkled with ashes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja pitää kovin huutaman sinun tähtes, ja vaikiasti valittaman, ja heittämän tomua päänsä päälle, ja tuhkaan itsensä tahraaman.
verses.king_james: And shall cause their voice to be heard against thee, and shall cry bitterly, and shall cast up dust upon their heads, they shall wallow themselves in the ashes:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et radent super te calvitium,  et accingentur ciliciis :  et plorabunt te in amaritudine animæ, ploratu amarissimo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִקְרִ֤יחוּ אֵלַ֙יִךְ֙ קׇרְחָ֔ה וְחָגְר֖וּ שַׂקִּ֑ים וּבָכ֥וּ אֵלַ֛יִךְ בְּמַר־נֶ֖פֶשׁ מִסְפֵּ֥ד מָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they shall shave themselves bald for thee, and shall be girded with haircloth: and they shall weep for thee with bitterness of soul, with most bitter weeping.
verses.finnish_biblia: Heidän pitää ajeleman hiukset päästänsä, ja pukeman säkit yllensä, ja sydämestänsä itkemän sinua, ja haikiasti murehtiman.
verses.king_james: And they shall make themselves utterly bald for thee, and gird them with sackcloth, and they shall weep for thee with bitterness of heart and bitter wailing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et assument super te carmen lugubre,  et plangent te :  Quæ est ut Tyrus, quæ obmutuit in medio maris ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְנָשְׂא֨וּ אֵלַ֤יִךְ בְּנִיהֶם֙ קִינָ֔ה וְקוֹנְנ֖וּ עָלָ֑יִךְ מִ֣י כְצ֔וֹר כְּדֻמָ֖ה בְּת֥וֹךְ הַיָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they shall take up a mournful song for thee, and shall lament thee: What city is like Tyre, which is become silent in the midst of the sea?
verses.finnish_biblia: Heidän pitää valituksessansa sinua itkemän ja surkutteleman: voi! kuka on ikänä niin pois tullut merellä kuin Tyro!
verses.king_james: And in their wailing they shall take up a lamentation for thee, and lament over thee, saying, What city is like Tyrus, like the destroyed in the midst of the sea?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ in exitu negotiationum tuarum de mari  implesti populos multos :  in multitudine divitiarum tuarum, et populorum tuorum,  ditasti reges terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּצֵ֤את עִזְבוֹנַ֙יִךְ֙ מִיַּמִּ֔ים הִשְׂבַּ֖עַתְּ עַמִּ֣ים רַבִּ֑ים בְּרֹ֤ב הוֹנַ֙יִךְ֙ וּמַ֣עֲרָבַ֔יִךְ הֶעֱשַׁ֖רְתְּ מַלְכֵי־אָֽרֶץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Which by thy merchandise that went from thee by sea didst fill many people: which by the multitude of thy riches, and of thy people didst enrich the kings of the earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin sinä kauppaa teit merellä, ravitsit sinä monta kansaa; ja sinun monella tavarallas ja kaupallas teit sinä maan kuninkaat äveriäiksi.
verses.king_james: When thy wares went forth out of the seas, thou filledst many people; thou didst enrich the kings of the earth with the multitude of thy riches and of thy merchandise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc contrita es a mari :  in profundis aquarum opes tuæ,  et omnis multitudo tua quæ erat in medio tui, ceciderunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֵ֛ת נִשְׁבֶּ֥רֶת מִיַּמִּ֖ים בְּמַֽעֲמַקֵּי־מָ֑יִם מַעֲרָבֵ֥ךְ וְכׇל־קְהָלֵ֖ךְ בְּתוֹכֵ֥ךְ נָפָֽלוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now thou art destroyed by the sea, thy riches are in the bottom of the waters, and all the multitude that was in the midst of thee is fallen.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta nyt sinä olet mereltä sysätty, juuri syvään veteen, niin että sinun kauppas ja kaikki sinun kansas sinussa on hukkunut.
verses.king_james: In the time when thou shalt be broken by the seas in the depths of the waters thy merchandise and all thy company in the midst of thee shall fall.

verses.latin_vulgate: Universi habitatores insularum obstupuerunt super te,  et reges earum omnes tempestate perculsi mutaverunt vultus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֚ל יֹשְׁבֵ֣י הָאִיִּ֔ים שָׁמְמ֖וּ עָלָ֑יִךְ וּמַלְכֵיהֶם֙ שָׂ֣עֲרוּ שַׂ֔עַר רָעֲמ֖וּ פָּנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: All the inhabitants of the islands are astonished at thee: and all their kings being struck with the storm have changed their countenance.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kaikki, jotka luodoissa asuvat, hämmästyvät sinua, ja heidän kuninkaansa tyhmistyvät ja katsovat murheellisesti.
verses.king_james: All the inhabitants of the isles shall be astonished at thee, and their kings shall be sore afraid, they shall be troubled in their countenance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Negotiatores populorum sibilaverunt super te :  ad nihilum deducta es,  et non eris usque in perpetuum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: סֹֽחֲרִים֙ בָּֽעַמִּ֔ים שָׁרְק֖וּ עָלָ֑יִךְ בַּלָּה֣וֹת הָיִ֔ית וְאֵינֵ֖ךְ עַד־עוֹלָֽם׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: The merchants of people have hissed at thee: thou art brought to nothing, and thou shalt never be any more.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kansain kauppamiehet viheltävät sinua, että sinä niin äkisti hävinnyt olet, ja et silleen enään taida tulla ylös ijankaikkisesti.
verses.king_james: The merchants among the people shall hiss at thee; thou shalt be a terror, and never shalt be any more.
